mainEntity.name: How much does a Range Rover Sport P400e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned P400e engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only. Fitting adds £900–£1,400 depending on the specialist. Used engines can be found from £2,500–£4,500, but condition and warranty vary. Note that battery replacement is a separate cost. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your Range Rover Sport.

mainEntity.name: Is the Range Rover Sport P400e engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Generally, yes — the 204PT petrol Ingenium is considered more reliable than its diesel counterpart. There's no timing chain tensioner catastrophe. The main issues are cooling system-related: water pump failure and coolant leaks. The long-term question is battery health — monitor it closely. With proper maintenance, it's a refined and powerful PHEV powertrain.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Range Rover Sport P400e?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most common issues are water pump failure (plastic impeller and seal degradation), coolant leaks from plastic hoses and thermostat housings, PHEV battery degradation over time, and occasional charging system faults. None are catastrophic if caught early — regular coolant system inspections and battery health checks are key.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover Sport P400e engine, or should I scrap the vehicle?

acceptedAnswer.text: Given the high retained value of the Range Rover Sport P400e (typical £25,000+ for a good example), a £5,000–£7,000 engine replacement is comfortably within the 'worth it' range. Our Land Rover scrap threshold is 60% of vehicle value — with a £6,000 engine cost against a £25,000 vehicle, it's a clear yes. However, factor in battery health if the vehicle is out of its 8-year warranty.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Range Rover Sport P400e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is 204PT, found on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation. It's the 2.0-litre turbocharged petrol Ingenium four-cylinder engine paired with an electric motor. If you're unsure, enter your registration on our site and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair a P400e water pump, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The pump must be replaced — it's a sealed unit with plastic impellers that degrade. A preventative replacement at 40,000 miles or at the first sign of leakage costs £450–£800. Ignoring it can lead to overheating and head gasket failure, which is far more expensive.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover Sport P400e — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Only if under warranty or vehicle has high value
Worth it on a £25k+ Range Rover Sport if the rest is sound
Replacement cost justified only on high-value examples

buyingChecks.title: Buying This P400e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full JLR service history with documented coolant system inspections
* Battery health check showing >85% state of health (or 8-year warranty still active)
* No signs of coolant loss or overheating (check coolant reservoir level)
* A 2019–2023 L494 facelift — the most refined PHEV Range Rover Sport to date
avoidIf: * Unexplained coolant loss with no visible leak — could indicate early water pump or head gasket issues
* Overheating history or low coolant level — risk of engine damage
* Significantly reduced electric range (indicates battery degradation)
* No battery health certificate or warranty transfer — assume battery is compromised

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The P400e is the plug-in hybrid Range Rover Sport facelift — a 2.0-litre four-cylinder petrol paired with an electric motor for a combined 398 bhp and 640 Nm torque. The petrol engine is significantly more reliable than the diesel Ingenium, with no timing chain catastrophe. The real long-term question is battery health — monitor it closely and ensure the 8-year warranty is transferable. With regular charging and careful thermal management, the P400e is a refined, powerful choice for the luxury SUV owner.

cta.label: → Compare P400e engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Sport P400e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for smooth idle; any hesitation or rough running points to injector or fuel pump issues
* Service History — Including coolant system inspections and hybrid service records — see the map above
* Coolant Level — Check reservoir when cold; any low or loss is a red flag
* Water Pump — Listen for whining noise; check for coolant residue near the pump area
* Electric Range — Compare displayed range against official figures; any major drop indicates battery degradation
* Charging System — Test both AC and DC charging; any errors or slow charging may indicate faults
* Battery Health — Request a battery health certificate (8-year warranty should guarantee 70% capacity)
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (PHEV is generally clean)
